title: centaurea × aliena
intro: Centaurea × aliena is a hybrid plant, resulting from the cross between Centaurea cyanus (cornflower) and Centaurea jacea (brown betony), and is also known as Knapweed or Centaurea. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which are attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea × aliena include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Centaurea × aliena can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including fever, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems.
